what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view C3 in area C, titled 'Demonstrate-first build-review card'; it records the journeys it belongs to, the ui:// addresses it surfaces, the components it builds to, and the design-lint and token sources used to produce it.
observations: id is C3, area is C, status is planned, platforms are desktop and mobile
title is 'Demonstrate-first build-review card' and it represents WIRE-review and INB-resolve
home_journey is J3; journeys lists J3 and J7; cross_journeys lists only J7
it enumerates nine ui:// addresses across activity, board, grow, inbox, and walkthrough scopes
build_to maps to components activity, board, grow, inbox, walkthrough
design_lint is 'check.py --target canvas/app/src --fail-on' described as the FORM gate
tokens are sourced from design/_system/tokens.json → design-system.css as the only look-source
the directory path follows a journey-spine (tree) layout: surfaces/J3/C3.json
the file explicitly warns that addresses must never be invented
standouts: The surface is framed as a 'Demonstrate-first build-review card' bridging review and resolve workflows
A single view is bound to two journeys (J3 home, J7 cross) via a concrete list of ui:// addresses
The build pipeline is pinned to a lint gate and a single token-to-CSS source
conventions: Uses a JSON surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, journeys, addresses, and build_to
Follows a journey-spine directory structure: surfaces/<home_journey>/<view_id>.json
References UI locations with a ui:// URI convention
Declares an empty symbols array because the file contains no code symbols
concerns: status is 'planned' while the file already contains generated build instructions and concrete addresses
J7 appears in both journeys and cross_journeys, but the in-file distinction is not defined
The design_lint string has a trailing space before the parenthetical and the tokens path uses an arrow character, which may confuse tooling
No generation timestamp or version is recorded for a generated artifact
symbols is empty despite the file referencing multiple components
notes: This is the planned surface spec for the C3 build-review/resolve card, mapping it to J3/J7 journeys, nine ui:// addresses, and a build pipeline with a lint gate and a single token source.
questions: What do the represent tags WIRE-review and INB-resolve specifically require of this surface?
How is cross_journeys semantically different from journeys, and why is J7 in both?
Which exact UI elements correspond to each ui:// address?
What does the FORM gate enforce and what happens when it fails?
Is this file fully generated or hand-edited, and how is it kept in sync with implementation?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a generated surface spec that records journeys, addresses, components, lint, and token sources for view C3.
apparent_themes: single source of truth for visual design via tokens
cross-journey reuse of a single view
intention_signals: It is trying to define a reusable build-review/resolve UI surface that can be referenced from multiple journeys, with explicit addresses and a deterministic build path from tokens through a lint gate.
novelty: The use of a ui:// URI namespace to enumerate surface addresses and the coupling of a planned view to a specific lint gate and single token source is distinctive; the journey-spine layout itself is conventional.
contribution: It contributes a structured, addressable surface definition for the C3 build-review/resolve card, including its journey membership, concrete ui:// addresses, target components, lint command, and token source, which a fused system could use to trace a view to its build inputs.
summary_for_embedding: A generated surface specification for view C3, the 'Demonstrate-first build-review card', in area C, planned for desktop and mobile, tied to journeys J3 and J7, listing nine ui:// addresses across activity, board, grow, inbox, and walkthrough components, and specifying a design-lint FORM gate and a single token-to-CSS source.
intention_for_embedding: To record the journeys, UI addresses, components, lint gate, and token source needed to build and review the C3 demonstrate-first build-review/resolve surface.
